name: Foreign/u.s. Equivalents
recipeInstructions:
AUSTRALIAN/AMERICAN EQUIVALENTS
1. Caster sugar: Superfine sugar
2. Demerara: Light brown sugar
3. Muscovado: Raw unrefined sugar
4. Sultanas: White raisins
5. Single Cream: Light cream
6. Double Cream: Heavy Cream
7. Bicarbonate of soda; Baking Soda
8. Digestive Biscuits; Graham Crackers
9. Biscuit or Cookie or Cracker
10. Minced meat; Ground meat
11. Aubergine; Eggplant
12. Beetroot; Beet
13. Courgette; Zucchini
14. Marrow: Large zucchini
15. Swede; Rutabaga
16. Porcini; Button Mushrooms
17. Mangetout; Snow Peas
18. 1 oz Chocolate = 1 square
19. 2/3 oz Bakers yeast = 1 cake
20. 3 tsp Dried yeast = 1 cake
Demerara: A brown sugar with separate grains, similar to Caribbean brown sugar. Substitute with light brown sugar if unavailable.
Muscovado: Very dark brown sugar (almost black), sticks together in a solid lump and is the rawest and tastiest of all sugars. Best for dark cakes and Christmas puddings. Large, varied brown crystals, hard and crunchy, typically used in coffee.
